title: cyphia linarioides
intro: Cyphia linarioides, also known as the Cyphian, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the eastern Himalayas and southern China. The Cyphian has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cyphia linarioides include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of Cyphia linarioides can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments in traditional medicine.
